chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP No.S-247 of 2025 ___________________________________________________ DATE: ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(S) OF JUDGE(S) ______________________________________________________ FRESH CASE. 1. For orders on CMA No.2187/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on Office Objections No.1 to 4, 9 to 12 and reply of Counsel as at ‘A’ 3. For orders on CMA No.2188/2025 (Exemption) 4. For hearing of Main Case 21-03-2025 M/s. Abdul Ghani Mangi, Ghulam Yaseen Mastoi and Ayaz Ahmed Solangi, Advocate for Petitioners a/w Petitioner Mst. Noor Nisssa and Khan Muhammad. ---------------- 1. Urgency granted. 2. Deferred. 3. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 4. Petitioners are present in Court along with their Advocates. Learned Counsel states that being sui juris Petitioner (Mst. Noor Nisssa) entered into marriage with (Khan Muhammad) with her own freewill and without any pressure against the wishes of her family members. In support of his submissions, the Counsel draws attention to the Nikahnama (at Court File Pg. 15) and Affidavit of Freewill (at Court File Pg. 19). Thereafter, her parents took her to village and assured that she will be given back to her husband namely Khan Muhammad but contrary today forceful Nikkah was solemnized with the Respondent No.6 which she never accepted. The Petitioner was under legal confinement of the Private Respondents and subjected the torture. She left her parents house and joined her husband/Petitioner No.2. The Private Respondents have declared them KARO KARI and are threatening the Petitioner and her husband with dire consequences. Petitioner - Mst.

The Private Respondents have declared them KARO KARI and are threatening the Petitioner and her husband with dire consequences. Petitioner - Mst. Noor Nisssa states that she has not been abducted by anyone but exercising right of choice, she has contracted freewill marriage with her husband – Khan Muhammad. Issue notice to the Respondents, Prosecutor General, Sindh as well as AAG for 14.4.2025. In the meanwhile, the official Respondents are directed to provide protection to the Petitioner and her husband in accordance with law. No adverse action shall be taken against the Petitioner and her husband. Copy of this order may be transmitted to the official Respondents through e-notice. JUDGE Jamil
